title: abrotanella filiformis
intro: Abrotanella filiformis, also known as the New Zealand helichrysum, is a flowering plant native to the alpine regions of New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce pain and inflammation. The plant has been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Abrotanella filiformis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
